{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sengen Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sengen Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

Sengen Kofun [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sengen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuji, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Sengen Kofun]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sengen Kofun]] [[en:Sengen Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|51|N|138|44|48|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fuji, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[Lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[Gakunan Railway]] [[Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/fujijikan/sp/fujipedia/kb719c0000001ksa.html Fuji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

=浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Sengen-kofun Panoramic view 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.16416666666667|N|138.74666666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fuji|WD=Q328613}} | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus is now crowned with a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a [[:en:lidar|lidar]] survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] [[:en:Kamiya Station|Kamiya Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

Per a {{ill|lidar|en|lidar|WD=Q504027|lt=lidar}} survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] {{ill|Kamiya Station|en|Kamiya Station|WD=Q6358616|lt=Kamiya Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] {{ill|Kamiya Station|en|Kamiya Station|WD=Q6358616|lt=Kamiya Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] {{ill|Kamiya Station|en|Kamiya Station|WD=Q6358616|lt=Kamiya Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}}

The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] {{ill|Kamiya Station|en|Kamiya Station|WD=Q6358616|lt=Kamiya Station|1=Kamiya Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sengen Kofun | native_name =浅間古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Sengen-kofun Panoramic view 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengen Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.16416666666667|N|138.74666666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fuji|WD=Q328613|1=Fuji, Shizuoka}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sengen Kofun'''|浅間古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Masukawa neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka|1=Fuji, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190892 |title=浅間古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengen Kofun is located on a gently sloping area at the foot of Mount Ashitaka. It is a "two conjoined rectangle" type ''kofun'' (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)) orientated to the southeast. Its total length is 97 meters and maximum width is 60 meters, making it the largest in eastern Shizuoka Prefecture. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 10 to 15 meters and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The tumulus is now crowned with a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but is assumed from its design to date to the late 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per a {{ill|lidar|en|lidar|WD=Q504027|lt=lidar|1=Lidar}} survey conducted in 2020, it was confirmed that the tumulus had two tiers in its posterior and a single tier in its anterior portion. rear and one step in the front. It became clear. A flat terrace protruding from the posterior portion, which is not visible to the naked eye was also found. The survey also reconfirmed that the height of the tumulus differs significantly between its north side (mountain side) and south side (sea side), making it clear that the tumulus is made to look larger when viewed from the sea side. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located approximately seven minutes on foot from the [[:en:Gakunan Railway|Gakunan Railway]] {{ill|Kamiya Station|en|Kamiya Station|WD=Q6358616|lt=Kamiya Station|1=Kamiya Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
